WGCNA Co-expression Network Analysis: Ewing Sarcoma (GSE17679)

Tumor-vs-Normal and Survival-Associated Module Discovery

Musfirah Tanveer

2026-07-19

## Allowing multi-threading with up to 4 threads.

Abstract

This analysis applies Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis (WGCNA) to GSE17679, an independent Ewing Sarcoma cohort (88 tumor samples, 18 normal skeletal muscle samples, Affymetrix HG-U133 Plus 2.0), to test whether NKX2-2 — previously identified as a diagnostic (DESeq2, GSE2553) and prognostic (Cox regression, GSE63157) biomarker — also holds up as a network-level hub gene in a third, independent dataset. Two complementary network analyses were performed: (1) tumor vs. normal tissue, and (2) a within-tumor comparison by vital status (Dead vs. No Evidence of Disease). NKX2-2 showed strong module membership in both networks (MM = 0.92 and MM = 0.53, respectively), and a within-tumor module enriched for cell-cycle/proliferation genes was found to correlate significantly with survival outcome (r = 0.36, p = 7×10⁻⁴).

1. Introduction

Ewing Sarcoma is a pediatric bone and soft-tissue malignancy driven by the EWSR1-FLI1 fusion oncogene. Prior work in this project series established NKX2-2, a homeodomain transcription factor, as (1) the top differentially upregulated gene distinguishing Ewing Sarcoma from other sarcoma subtypes (DESeq2 on GSE2553), (2) a significant prognostic marker of overall and event-free survival (Cox regression on GSE63157), and (3) the top hub gene of an Ewing-specific, GO-enriched co-expression module (WGCNA on GSE2553, restricted to 5 major sarcoma subtypes).

This analysis extends that evidence base to a fourth, fully independent cohort (GSE17679) and a different analytical framing — network topology rather than group-mean comparison — to test whether NKX2-2’s centrality replicates outside the original dataset.

2. Methods

2.1 Data Source & Sample Selection

GSE17679 (Affymetrix HG-U133 Plus 2.0, GPL570) was downloaded via GEOquery. Of 117 total samples, 11 in vitro cultured cell line samples were excluded (systematically different expression from primary tissue due to absence of tumor microenvironment). The remaining 106 samples (88 primary Ewing Sarcoma tumor, 18 normal skeletal muscle) were retained for network construction.

2.2 Quality Control

Expression data were confirmed already log2/RMA-normalized (comparable medians across samples on visual inspection). Data were transposed to WGCNA’s required samples-as-rows orientation and passed through goodSamplesGenes() prior to any network construction. Sample outliers were assessed via average-linkage hierarchical clustering on Euclidean distance.

2.3 Network Construction

For each of the two comparisons (tumor-vs-normal; within-tumor survival status), analysis proceeded independently: gene filtering to the top 5,000 most-variable probes, soft-thresholding power selection via pickSoftThreshold() targeting scale-free topology fit R² > 0.85 (signed network), module detection via blockwiseModules() (networkType = "signed", TOMType = "signed", minModuleSize = 30, mergeCutHeight = 0.25), and module eigengene correlation against the relevant trait. set.seed(1234) was fixed prior to each network build for reproducibility. Hub genes were identified by module membership (correlation of each gene to its module eigengene) and mapped to gene symbols via the hgu133plus2.db annotation package. Modules were selected for downstream reporting dynamically (by strength of trait correlation), not by hardcoded module number, to avoid reproducibility issues from run-to-run module relabeling.

2.4 Enrichment

GO Biological Process enrichment (2023 release) was performed on the top 200 module-member genes via enrichR.

3. Results — Analysis 1: Tumor vs. Normal Tissue

3.1 Sample Quality Control

Figure 1: Sample clustering colored by tissue type (red = tumor, blue = normal)

Sample clustering (Figure 1) shows a clean, expected split: normal muscle samples separate from tumor samples at a high fusion height, consistent with real biological signal rather than a technical artifact. No samples were removed.

Figure 2: Scale-free topology fit and mean connectivity across candidate powers (tumor vs. normal network)

Power 18 was selected as the lowest power at which R² clearly exceeded 0.85 (R² = 0.917) with acceptable mean connectivity (74.3). Signed networks typically require higher powers (12-30) than unsigned networks (6-10) to reach equivalent fit, which this result is consistent with.

3.3 Network Construction & Modules

Table 1: Module sizes, tumor-vs-normal network
Module N_genes
0 (unassigned/grey) 526
1 2261
2 1082
3 739
4 254
5 80
6 58

Figure 3: Gene dendrogram and module assignment (tumor vs. normal network)

The unassigned (grey) module comprised 10.5% of genes, within the expected healthy range (10-20%), indicating the network found real co-expression structure.

3.4 Module-Trait Correlation

Figure 4: Module-trait relationships, tumor vs. normal tissue

Two modules showed very strong correlation with tumor status: ME1 (r = 0.95, p = 1.9×10⁻⁵⁶, tumor-associated) and ME2 (r = −0.98, p = 1.3×10⁻⁷⁰, normal-tissue-associated). Both survive Bonferroni correction for 7 modules tested (α ≈ 0.007).

NKX2-2 (probe 206915_at) had module membership MM = 0.918 in ME1, ranking 166 of 2261 genes (top 7.3%) — a strong, hub-range correlation, though not the module’s top-ranked gene.

Table 4: GO Biological Process enrichment, ME1
Term P.value Adjusted.P.value
Chromatin Organization (GO:0006325) 0.0001953 0.0722975
mRNA Processing (GO:0006397) 0.0002161 0.0722975
Positive Regulation Of Viral Process (GO:0048524) 0.0002481 0.0722975
Regulation Of DNA-templated Transcription Elongation (GO:0032784) 0.0003268 0.0722975
Late Endosome To Vacuole Transport (GO:0045324) 0.0004292 0.0734597
Focal Adhesion Assembly (GO:0048041) 0.0004980 0.0734597
Endosome Transport Via Multivesicular Body Sorting Pathway (GO:0032509) 0.0007455 0.0827795
RNA Polyadenylation (GO:0043631) 0.0008425 0.0827795
Regulation Of RNA Splicing (GO:0043484) 0.0010309 0.0827795
mRNA Splicing, Via Spliceosome (GO:0000398) 0.0010852 0.0827795

ME1’s top hub genes (RPL3, EEF1A1, TUBB, MYC, TP53) reflect general ribosomal/translation and cell-cycle machinery rather than Ewing-specific biology, and enrichment did not reach adjusted significance (all adjusted p > 0.05). This is consistent with ME1 capturing a broad tumor-vs-normal proliferative-state signature rather than disease-specific identity — an expected pattern when the comparison is tumor vs. any normal tissue rather than tumor subtype vs. subtype.

4. Results — Analysis 2: Within-Tumor Survival Status

Because the tumor-vs-normal network above is dominated by the large tissue-type signal, a second network was built using only the 88 tumor samples, to allow finer within-tumor co-expression structure — related to clinical outcome — to emerge.

4.1 Cohort & Trait

Vital status (status:ch1) was used as a binary trait: Dead (n=52) vs. No Evidence of Disease (NED, n=34). Samples with intermediate “Alive With Disease” status (n=2) were excluded as too few for reliable grouping.

4.2 Soft-Thresholding Power & Network Construction

##    Power SFT.R.sq   slope truncated.R.sq mean.k. median.k. max.k.
## 1      1 2.74e-02  2.4800          0.889 2660.00   2650.00   3050

## 3      3 2.32e-02 -0.6900          0.820  868.00    831.00   1360
## 4      4 1.01e-01 -0.9950          0.767  531.00    492.00    985
## 5      5 2.52e-01 -1.2100          0.725  339.00    300.00    740
## 6      6 5.22e-01 -1.4300          0.751  225.00    190.00    574
## 7      7 7.33e-01 -1.5900          0.840  154.00    124.00    462
## 8      8 8.21e-01 -1.6600          0.880  109.00     83.40    383
## 9      9 8.55e-01 -1.7000          0.902   79.50     56.90    323
## 10    10 8.67e-01 -1.7000          0.912   59.40     39.70    276

## 13    16 8.60e-01 -1.7000          0.944   15.30      6.26    127
## 14    18 8.56e-01 -1.7000          0.955   10.90      3.81    102
## 15    20 8.50e-01 -1.7000          0.956    7.99      2.44     83

Power 9 was selected (R² = 0.855, mean connectivity = 79.5). The somewhat lower plateau R² compared to the tumor-vs-normal network (0.85-0.87 vs. 0.92) is expected for a more homogeneous, single-tissue- type cohort, where no single dominant signal drives network structure.

Grey module comprised 14.1% of genes — again within the healthy range.

4.3 Module-Trait Correlation (Survival Status)

Figure 5: Module-trait relationships, vital status (Dead vs. NED)

Four modules survived Bonferroni correction (α ≈ 0.0038 for 13 modules): ME6 (r = −0.48, p = 2.6×10⁻⁶), ME2 (r = −0.46, p = 7.3×10⁻⁶), ME3 (r = 0.36, p = 7.1×10⁻⁴), and ME8 (r = −0.35, p = 9.3×10⁻⁴). Negative correlations indicate modules elevated in NED (surviving) patients; the positive ME3 correlation indicates a module elevated in patients who died.

ME3 is a clean, textbook mitotic/cell-cycle module (SPAG5, CENPF, KIF2C, BUB1B, BUB1, MCM2, CENPA) — higher activity of this proliferation program predicts worse survival, a biologically expected finding in cancer prognosis broadly.

NKX2-2 landed in ME2 (ME2), the module most protective against death, with MM = 0.529, ranked 390 of 746 genes (top 52.3%) — above the conventional hub-gene threshold (MM > 0.5).

Table 8: GO Biological Process enrichment, ME2
Term P.value Adjusted.P.value
Adrenergic Receptor Signaling Pathway (GO:0071875) 0.0000001 0.0000922
Positive Regulation Of Heart Rate (GO:0010460) 0.0000038 0.0020378
Adenylate Cyclase-Activating Adrenergic Receptor Signaling Pathway (GO:0071880) 0.0001153 0.0412426
Regulation Of Glucose Transmembrane Transport (GO:0010827) 0.0001707 0.0457903
Protein Localization To Membrane (GO:0072657) 0.0002632 0.0564905
Protein Localization To Cell Periphery (GO:1990778) 0.0003339 0.0597146
Negative Regulation Of Establishment Of Protein Localization To Mitochondrion (GO:1903748) 0.0006182 0.0838775
Regulation Of Vasoconstriction (GO:0019229) 0.0006254 0.0838775
Adenylate Cyclase-Activating G Protein-Coupled Receptor Signaling Pathway (GO:0007189) 0.0007620 0.0908456
Positive Regulation Of Microtubule Polymerization (GO:0031116) 0.0008665 0.0929722

Figure 6: Top enriched GO Biological Process terms, ME2

ME2 was significantly enriched (adjusted p < 0.05) for adrenergic receptor signaling and heart rate regulation, and near-significant for glucose transport and protein localization to membrane — a signature more consistent with stromal/vascular or neuroendocrine-adjacent signaling than tumor-intrinsic proliferation. This is plausibly consistent with NKX2-2’s known role in neuroendocrine differentiation programs, though should be treated as exploratory given several terms sit in the 0.04-0.09 adjusted-p range.

5. Discussion

This analysis provides a fourth independent line of evidence for NKX2-2’s relevance in Ewing Sarcoma biology, following diagnostic (DESeq2, GSE2553), prognostic (Cox regression, GSE63157), and network-topology (WGCNA, GSE2553) evidence from prior work in this project series:

  • In an independent cohort (GSE17679) and independent array platform, NKX2-2 showed strong module membership (MM = 0.92, top 7%) in the dominant tumor-vs-normal module, though that module’s biology reflected general proliferation/translation machinery rather than a disease-specific signature (GO enrichment non-significant).
  • In a refined, within-tumor analysis using vital status, NKX2-2 showed significant, moderate hub-gene-range membership (MM = 0.53) in a GO-enriched, survival-protective module (ME2, r = −0.46, p = 7.3×10⁻⁶ with death).
  • An independent, strongly significant proliferation module (ME3) was also identified as predictive of survival (r = 0.36, p = 7.1×10⁻⁴), representing a standalone finding of value regardless of NKX2-2’s involvement.

These results should be read as corroborating, moderate-strength evidence rather than a replication of the sharper, top-hub-gene result obtained on GSE2553 (where NKX2-2 was module ME14’s single strongest member, r = 0.94 with subtype). The difference is expected: GSE2553’s WGCNA compared Ewing against other sarcoma subtypes, a comparison that directly isolates Ewing-specific identity genes, whereas GSE17679’s primary comparison (tumor vs. normal muscle) is dominated by broader “malignant vs. non-malignant” signal. The survival-status refinement within GSE17679 recovers a sharper, biologically interpretable signal more comparable to the GSE2553 result.

6. Conclusion

Across four independent analyses spanning three GEO datasets and two analytical methods (differential expression, network co-expression), NKX2-2 consistently shows strong, statistically robust association with Ewing Sarcoma identity and, more moderately, with survival outcome. This convergence across independent cohorts and methods supports NKX2-2 as a genuine biological feature of Ewing Sarcoma rather than a dataset-specific artifact, and provides a solid multi-method evidence base for Paper 2.

References

  1. GSE17679: NCBI GEO
  2. WGCNA: Langfelder P, Horvath S. BMC Bioinformatics (2008)
  3. enrichR: Kuleshov MV et al. Nucleic Acids Research (2016)
  4. R Core Team (2024)
